text:
BOTTOM LINE — Oil surged, tech sank, small caps held. The tape was a commodity-led rotation triggered by US blockade restart, pushing oil +3.91%, while SOXX -1.64% and QQQ -1.50% confirmed a growth-to-value shift with defensive Treasuries bid, a classic supply-shock trade.

INDICES — VIX 18.8, up 0.18 points. IWM -0.52%, SPY -0.99%, DIA -0.77%, QQQ -1.50%, SOXX -1.64%. Small > large by 0.47pp. Cross-asset: USO +3.91%, TLT +0.37%, UUP -0.04%.

VOLUME — SPY vol_ratio 1.0x, QQQ 1.1x — average. SOXX 1.7x and RSP 1.6x gained attention, indicating distribution in semis and accumulation in equal-weight. XLP 1.5x and XLY 1.4x saw elevated volume with negative prices, signaling distribution in consumer staples and discretionary.

SECTORS & ROTATION — XLE Energy +1.16%, XLRE Real Estate -0.09%, XLI Industrials -0.41%, XLV Healthcare -0.44%, XLU Utilities -0.66%, XLB Materials -0.71%, XLP Staples -0.72%, XLF Financials -0.86%, XLK Technology -1.09%, XLY Discretionary -1.62%, XLC Comms -1.78%. Top industries: USO Oil +3.91%, XOP Oil & Gas +2.25%. Bottom: JETS Airlines -2.53%, IHI Medtech -3.24%. Money out of tech and discretionary into energy and energy-linked industries. Rotation state: oil-led rotation.

POSITIONING — SPY put-call ratio 0.79 near complacency, QQQ 1.03 elevated protection demand. QQQ skew +12.7% signals fear in tech. VIX 18.8 above its 20-day MA of 17.0, indicating elevated fear. IWM PCR_vol 4.15 extreme protection demand.

CROSS-ASSET — Oil +3.91% as US restarts blockade, pressuring airlines JETS -2.53% and lifting energy XLE +1.16%. Gold +0.95% despite risk-off, unusual — safe-haven bid plus supply-shock inflation hedge.

SIGNALS & WATCH — Fear & Greed Neutral 59.8. Top movers: TRV +9.22% (Insurance), SQM +5.25% (New Metals), ISRG -14.15% (Medtech). Best 20d momentum: XLE +4.19%. Worst: SLV -19.89%. Vol spikes: ISRG 4.3x vol, -14.15% — distribution in medtech. NFLX 2.9x vol, -7.26% — distribution after Q3 revenue miss. Watch: TRV +9.22% — vol spike, Insurance leaders; ISRG -14.15% — vol spike near 200-day SMA, distribution; XOP +2.25% — oil sector momentum, follow-through on supply shock.
